evidence: Selected action play-single-spade-A-copy2 is in legalActions.
Lead actions do not need to beat an existing table lead.
All cited evidence ids are present in public history.

Trace asserts hidden information without uncertainty markers: Leading the spade A single is stronger than leading the spade 2. If the A wins the trick, player 0 retains the lead and can exit the low 2 on the next turn. If an opponent overtakes, the partner still has a chance to regain the lead. Leading the 2 first would almost certainly lose the trick immediately, leaving player 0 with only the A and no guaranteed path to play it.
Partner consistency needs a public scenario tag before this conservative check applies.
Opponent consistency needs a public scenario tag before this conservative check applies.
Trace rationale is compatible with the selected action type.
finish_hand is compatible with playing from an endgame hand.
